page: Microsoft Azure EC20as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECACCV5 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 10 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The CPU features 32 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2702.38. Memory subsystem performance is also strong, as reflected by the PassMark memory score of 2819.58. Multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core (11393.0) and PassMark CPU (26792.66), falls within the average range. Large-block memory bandwidth reaches 78.98 GB/sec for read operations and 44.56 GB/sec for write operations. In language model inference, the server achieves top-tier performance for text generation using a small 135M model (326.34 tokens/sec), though it struggles with large 70B models, recording a poor prompt processing speed of 4.16 tokens/sec.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU and dedicated AMD EPYC hardware, the EC20as_cc_v5 offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing for flexible remote storage configurations. This server is well-suited for memory-heavy applications such as relational databases, caching layers like Redis (which benchmarked at 20.28 million operations per second), medium-scale data processing, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure EC20as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating at 3.525 GHz, it provides 20 vCPUs (10 physical cores) and 160.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. Notably, it delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models (326.34 tokens/sec) but exhibits poor performance on large 70B models. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. It is highly suited for database deployments, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC20as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 20 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 160 GB RAM. It delivers strong single-core and memory performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC20as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density, dedicated AMD EPYC processors, and strong single-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 20 vCPUs and 10 physical cores
160.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
Strong single-core CPU and memory subsystem benchmark performance
No local storage or GPUs, optimized for remote Premium Storage
Suited for databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive applications
